req.title: Audit log access (boundary)
req.statement: A privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation; cross-organisation access shall be forbidden. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
preconditions: role:privacy-officer

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-039 requires three distinct partitions: (1) a privacy-officer querying their own org — valid, should return 200 with scoped records; (2) a privacy-officer querying a foreign org — invalid, must return 403; (3) boundary values on the timestamp/_count query parameters — e.g. _count=0 (lower bound), _count=1 (first valid), and an epoch-edge timestamp. EP covers the happy-path org-scoped query; NEG covers cross-org access; BVA covers numeric and timestamp boundaries. A DT case is added to cover the decision matrix of (auth role) x (org match) combinations. State-transition is less applicable here but is noted where token state (valid vs expired) transitions are exercised.

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_reads_own_org_audit_events
preconditions: User has role:privacy-officer for org-A
At least one AuditEvent record exists scoped to org-A
Valid JWT bearer token for privacy-officer is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A via <get_token_fixture>
GET /AuditEvent?organization=org-A with Authorization header
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response body is a FHIR Bundle
trace: EP: valid input partition — officer queries own org
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir"

def test_privacy_officer_reads_own_org_audit_events(get_token, org_audit_seed):
    token = get_token(role="privacy-officer", org="org-A")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": "org-A"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1
    for entry in bundle.get("entry", []):
        resource = entry["resource"]
        agents = resource.get("agent", [])
        refs = [a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "") for a in agents]
        assert any("org-A" in r for r in refs), f"Cross-org record leaked: {refs}"

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_forbidden_from_cross_org_audit_events
preconditions: User has role:privacy-officer for org-A only
AuditEvent records exist scoped to org-B
Valid JWT bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A via <get_token_fixture>
GET /AuditEvent?organization=org-B with Authorization header
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.severity='error'
No AuditEvent resources from org-B are returned
trace: R-HLT-039: cross-organisation access shall be forbidden
R-HLT-039: error pathway — 403 when crossing org boundary
NEG: invalid input partition — officer queries foreign org
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir"

def test_privacy_officer_forbidden_from_cross_org_audit_events(get_token):
    token = get_token(role="privacy-officer", org="org-A")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": "org-B"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues)

tests.name: test_audit_event_boundary_count_zero_returns_empty_bundle
preconditions: User has role:privacy-officer for org-A
AuditEvent records exist for org-A
Valid JWT bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A via <get_token_fixture>
GET /AuditEvent?organization=org-A&_count=0 with Authorization header
Capture HTTP status, total, and entry count
expected: Bundle.total reflects actual record count (server-side knowledge)
Bundle.entry is absent or empty — zero resources returned in page
trace: R-HLT-039: boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested
BVA: lower boundary of _count parameter — 0 yields empty page
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir"

def test_audit_event_boundary_count_zero_returns_empty_bundle(get_token, org_audit_seed):
    token = get_token(role="privacy-officer", org="org-A")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": "org-A", "_count": "0"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 0, f"Expected 0 entries with _count=0, got {len(entries)}"

tests.name: test_audit_event_boundary_count_one_returns_single_record
preconditions: User has role:privacy-officer for org-A
At least two AuditEvent records exist for org-A
Valid JWT bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A via <get_token_fixture>
GET /AuditEvent?organization=org-A&_count=1 with Authorization header
Capture HTTP status and entry count
expected: The single entry is scoped to org-A
trace: R-HLT-039: boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested
BVA: first-valid boundary of _count — 1 yields exactly one record
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir"

def test_audit_event_boundary_count_one_returns_single_record(get_token, org_audit_seed):
    token = get_token(role="privacy-officer", org="org-A")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": "org-A", "_count": "1"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 1, f"Expected exactly 1 entry, got {len(entries)}"
    resource = entries[0]["resource"]
    agents = resource.get("agent", [])
    refs = [a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "") for a in agents]
    assert any("org-A" in r for r in refs)

tests.name: test_audit_event_boundary_timestamp_epoch_start
preconditions: User has role:privacy-officer for org-A
AuditEvent records exist for org-A with recorded dates after 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Valid JWT bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A via <get_token_fixture>
GET /AuditEvent?organization=org-A&date=ge1970-01-01T00:00:00Z with Authorization header
Capture HTTP status and total
expected: total >= 0 (no server crash on epoch boundary)
trace: R-HLT-039: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
BVA: minimum timestamp boundary — Unix epoch lower bound
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import timezone, datetime

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir"
EPOCH = "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"

def test_audit_event_boundary_timestamp_epoch_start(get_token):
    token = get_token(role="privacy-officer", org="org-A")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": "org-A", "date": f"ge{EPOCH}"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    assert "total" in bundle
    assert bundle["total"] >= 0

tests.name: test_decision_table_non_privacy_officer_role_forbidden
preconditions: User has role:clinician for org-A (not privacy-officer)
AuditEvent records exist for org-A
Valid JWT bearer token for clinician is available
actions: Obtain bearer token for clinician of org-A via <get_token_fixture>
GET /AuditEvent?organization=org-A with Authorization header
Capture HTTP status
expected: No AuditEvent resources exposed to clinician role
trace: R-HLT-039: privacy-officer is the sole authorised actor
DT: decision cell — wrong role + correct org => 403
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir"

def test_decision_table_non_privacy_officer_role_forbidden(get_token):
    token = get_token(role="clinician", org="org-A")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": "org-A"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    severities = [i.get("severity") for i in issues]
    assert "error" in severities or "fatal" in severities

tests.name: test_state_transition_expired_token_returns_401
preconditions: An expired JWT bearer token for privacy-officer of org-A is available
AuditEvent records exist for org-A
actions: Use an expired token obtained via <expired_token_fixture>
GET /AuditEvent?organization=org-A with expired Authorization header
Capture HTTP status
expected: Response indicates token expiry (WWW-Authenticate header or OperationOutcome)
trace: R-HLT-039: precondition is role:privacy-officer with valid session
STC: token state transition from valid -> expired -> rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/fhir"

def test_state_transition_expired_token_returns_401(expired_token):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {expired_token}"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"organization": "org-A"},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    assert "entry" not in body, "AuditEvent data must not be present with expired token"
